1→package cmd
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"syscall"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/sandcastle/cli/api"
     8→	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
     9→	"golang.org/x/term"
    10→)
    11→
    12→func init() {
    13→	smbCmd.AddCommand(smbSetPasswordCmd)
    14→	rootCmd.AddCommand(smbCmd)
    15→}
    16→
    17→var smbCmd = &cobra.Command{
    18→	Use:   "smb",
    19→	Short: "Manage SMB file sharing",
    20→	Long: `Manage SMB (Samba) file sharing for your sandboxes.
    21→
    22→SMB lets you mount your sandbox home directory or /persisted as a network drive
    23→from macOS, Windows, or Linux. Requires Tailscale for access.
    24→
    25→Enable SMB when creating a sandbox with --smb, then connect via:
    26→  macOS:   smb://<tailscale-ip>/home  or  smb://<tailscale-ip>/persisted
    27→  Windows: \\<tailscale-ip>\home
    28→  Linux:   mount -t cifs //<tailscale-ip>/home /mnt -o username=<you>`,
    29→}
    30→
    31→var smbSetPasswordCmd = &cobra.Command{
    32→	Use:   "set-password",
    33→	Short: "Set your SMB password for file sharing",
    34→	Long: `Set the SMB password used to authenticate when mounting sandbox shares.
    35→
    36→The password is stored encrypted on the server and passed to sandboxes
    37→that have SMB enabled. All your SMB-enabled sandboxes share the same password.`,
    38→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    39→		client, err := api.NewClient()
    40→		if err != nil {
    41→			return err
    42→		}
    43→		printServer(client)
    44→
    45→		fmt.Print("New SMB password: ")
    46→		pass1, err := term.ReadPassword(syscall.Stdin)
    47→		fmt.Println()
    48→		if err != nil {
    49→			return fmt.Errorf("reading password: %w", err)
    50→		}
    51→
    52→		fmt.Print("Confirm SMB password: ")
    53→		pass2, err := term.ReadPassword(syscall.Stdin)
    54→		fmt.Println()
    55→		if err != nil {
    56→			return fmt.Errorf("reading password: %w", err)
    57→		}
    58→
    59→		if string(pass1) != string(pass2) {
    60→			return fmt.Errorf("passwords do not match")
    61→		}
    62→
    63→		if len(pass1) == 0 {
    64→			return fmt.Errorf("password cannot be empty")
    65→		}
    66→
    67→		if err := client.SmbSetPassword(string(pass1)); err != nil {
    68→			return fmt.Errorf("setting SMB password: %w", err)
    69→		}
    70→
    71→		fmt.Println("SMB password updated. Active SMB-enabled sandboxes have been updated.")
    72→		return nil
    73→	},
    74→}

1→class DashboardController < ApplicationController
     2→  def index
     3→    @sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)
     4→    @archived_sandboxes = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.includes(:routes).order(:name)
     5→    @vnc_active_ids = running_vnc_ids
     6→    @tailscale_ips = tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)
     7→  end
     8→
     9→  def card
    10→    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).find(params[:id])
    11→    authorize sandbox
    12→    vnc_active = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    13→    tailscale_ip = sandbox.tailscale? ? TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox) : nil
    14→    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(helpers.dom_id(sandbox), partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: sandbox, vnc_active: vnc_active, tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip })
    15→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    16→    # Sandbox was destroyed/archived while the dashboard was open — remove the card
    17→    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove("sandbox_#{params[:id]}")
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  def stats
    21→    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    22→    authorize sandbox, :stats?
    23→
    24→    if sandbox.status == "running" && sandbox.container_id.present?
    25→      @stats = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
    26→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    27→        raw = container.stats(stream: false)
    28→
    29→        next nil if raw.blank?
    30→
    31→        networks = raw["networks"] || {}
    32→        net_rx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["rx_bytes"] || 0 }
    33→        net_tx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["tx_bytes"] || 0 }
    34→
    35→        blkio = raw.dig("blkio_stats", "io_service_bytes_recursive") || []
    36→        disk_read = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "read" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }
    37→        disk_write = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "write" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }
    38→
    39→        {
    40→          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
    41→          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
    42→          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
    43→          net_rx: net_rx,
    44→          net_tx: net_tx,
    45→          disk_read: disk_read,
    46→          disk_write: disk_write,
    47→          pids: raw.dig("pids_stats", "current") || 0
    48→        }
    49→      end
    50→    end
    51→
    52→    respond_to do |format|
    53→      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: @stats, sandbox: sandbox } }
    54→      format.json { render json: @stats ? { cpu: @stats[:cpu_percent], mem: @stats[:memory_mb].round(1) } : { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    55→    end
    56→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    57→    respond_to do |format|
    58→      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: nil } }
    59→      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    60→    end
    61→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    62→    respond_to do |format|
    63→      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: sandbox } }
    64→      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    65→    end
    66→  end
    67→
    68→  private
    69→
    70→  def running_vnc_ids
    71→    Dir.glob(File.join(VncManager::DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-*.yml")).filter_map { |f|
    72→      File.basename(f).match(/\Avnc-(\d+)\.yml\z/)&.[](1)&.to_i
    73→    }.to_set
    74→  rescue
    75→    Set.new
    76→  end
    77→
    78→  def tailscale_ips_for(sandboxes)
    79→    ts_sandboxes = sandboxes.select { |s| s.tailscale? && s.container_id.present? }
    80→    return {} if ts_sandboxes.empty?
    81→
    82→    all_containers = Docker::Container.all
    83→    container_map = all_containers.each_with_object({}) { |c, h| h[c.id] = c }
    84→
    85→    ts_sandboxes.each_with_object({}) do |sandbox, ips|
    86→      container = container_map[sandbox.container_id]
    87→      next unless container
    88→      network = sandbox.user.tailscale_network
    89→      next unless network.present?
    90→      ip = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", network, "IPAddress")
    91→      ips[sandbox.id] = ip if ip.present?
    92→    end
    93→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    94→    {}
    95→  end
    96→end
    97→

1→class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
     2→  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts
     3→
     4→  belongs_to :user
     5→  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
     6→  has_many :container_metrics, dependent: :delete_all
     7→
     8→  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
     9→  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze
    10→
    11→  validates :name, presence: true,
    12→    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) } }
    13→  validates :name, format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" },
    14→    unless: -> { status.in?(%w[destroyed archived]) }
    15→  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed archived] }
    16→  validates :image, presence: true
    17→  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
    18→  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }
    19→  validate :smb_prerequisites, if: -> { smb_enabled? }
    20→
    21→  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) }
    22→  scope :archived, -> { where(status: "archived") }
    23→  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }
    24→
    25→  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
    26→  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    27→  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    28→  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    29→
    30→  def full_name
    31→    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def connect_command
    35→    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  def routed?
    39→    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  def temp?
    43→    temporary?
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  def smb_enabled?
    47→    smb_enabled
    48→  end
    49→
    50→  # Job lifecycle management
    51→  def job_in_progress?
    52→    job_status.present?
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  def job_failed?
    56→    job_error.present?
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def start_job(status)
    60→    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
    61→  end
    62→
    63→  def finish_job
    64→    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
    65→  end
    66→
    67→  def fail_job(error_message)
    68→    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
    69→  end
    70→
    71→  private
    72→
    73→  def smb_prerequisites
    74→    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires Tailscale to be enabled") unless user&.tailscale_enabled?
    75→    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires an SMB password to be set in Settings") unless user&.smb_password.present?
    76→  end
    77→
    78→  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    79→    broadcast_prepend_to(
    80→      [ user, "dashboard" ],
    81→      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
    82→      locals: { sandbox: self },
    83→      target: "sandboxes"
    84→    )
    85→  rescue => e
    86→    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_prepend: #{e.message}")
    87→  end
    88→
    89→  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    90→    # If sandbox is destroyed or archived, remove it from the active dashboard
    91→    if status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
    92→      broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
    93→    else
    94→      broadcast_replace_to(
    95→        [ user, "dashboard" ],
    96→        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
    97→        locals: { sandbox: self },
    98→        target: dom_id(self)
    99→      )
   100→    end
   101→  rescue => e
   102→    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_replace: #{e.message}")
   103→  end
   104→
   105→  def broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
   106→    broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
   107→  rescue => e
   108→    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_remove: #{e.message}")
   109→  end
   110→end

     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      mount_home: mount_home,
    15→      data_path: data_path,
    16→      temporary: temporary
    17→    )
    18→
    19→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    20→    sandbox.validate!
    21→
    22→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    23→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    24→
    25→    # Now safe to save
    26→    sandbox.save!
    27→
    28→    # Pull image
    29→    ensure_image(image)
    30→
    31→    # Record image digest and build date
    32→    info = fetch_image_info(image)
    33→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at])
    34→
    35→    # Create and start container
    36→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    37→
    38→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    39→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    40→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    41→    end
    42→
    43→    sandbox
    44→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    45→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    46→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    47→  rescue => e
    48→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    49→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    50→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    51→    raise Error, e.message
    52→  end

    54→  # Public method for job usage
    55→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    56→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    57→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    58→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    59→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    60→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    61→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    62→      "HostConfig" => {
    63→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    64→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    65→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    66→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    67→      },
    68→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    69→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    70→      }
    71→    )
    72→
    73→    container.start
    74→    container.refresh!
    75→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    76→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    77→      container.stop rescue nil
    78→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    79→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    80→    end
    81→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    82→
    83→    # Connect to per-user network for tenant isolation
    84→    begin
    85→      NetworkManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    86→    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
    87→      container.stop rescue nil
    88→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    89→      sandbox.update!(container_id: nil, status: "pending")
    90→      raise Error, "Failed to attach tenant network: #{e.message}"
    91→    end
    92→
    93→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    94→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    95→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    96→
    97→    # Set SMB password via exec (avoids leaking password in container env/metadata)
    98→    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?
    99→  end

   101→  # Public method for job usage
   102→  def ensure_image(image)
   103→    Docker::Image.get(image)
   104→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   105→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
   106→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
   107→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   108→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
   109→  end

   111→  # Public method for job usage
   112→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   113→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   114→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   115→
   116→    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   117→    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   118→
   119→    if sandbox.mount_home
   120→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   121→      ensure_dir(dir)
   122→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   123→    end
   124→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   125→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   126→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   127→
   128→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   129→      ensure_dir(dir)
   130→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   131→    end
   132→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   133→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   134→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   135→      ensure_dir(dir)
   136→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   137→    end
   138→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   139→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   140→  end

   186→  end

   283→  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
   284→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   285→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   286→
   287→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288→    user = sandbox.user.name
   289→
   290→    case service
   291→    when "docker"
   292→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
   293→    when "vnc"
   294→      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
   295→      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
   296→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
   297→    end
   298→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   299→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   300→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   301→    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
   302→  end
   303→
   304→  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
   305→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   306→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   307→
   308→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   309→
   310→    case service
   311→    when "docker"
   312→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   313→    when "vnc"
   314→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   315→    end
   316→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   317→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   318→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e

   642→  # Update the SMB password on all active SMB-enabled sandboxes for a user.
   643→  def update_smb_password(user:)
   644→    raise Error, "No SMB password set" unless user.smb_password.present?
   645→
   646→    user.sandboxes.active.where(smb_enabled: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   647→      next if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   648→
   649→      begin
   650→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   651→        set_smb_password(container, user)
   652→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   653→        # Container gone, skip
   654→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   655→        Rails.logger.warn("Failed to update SMB password for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
   656→      end
   657→    end
   658→  end

   662→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   663→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   664→    max_attempts.times do
   665→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   666→
   667→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   668→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   669→        sleep delay
   670→        next
   671→      end
   672→
   673→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   674→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   675→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   676→
   677→      sleep delay
   678→    end
   679→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   680→    nil
   681→  end

   809→  # Inject SMB password into the container via exec, avoiding env var leakage.
   810→  def set_smb_password(container, user)
   811→    return unless user.smb_password.present?
   812→
   813→    username = user.name
   814→    password = user.smb_password
   815→    container.exec(
   816→      ["bash", "-c", "printf '%s\\n%s\\n' \"$0\" \"$0\" | smbpasswd -a -s \"$1\" 2>&1 || echo 'Warning: smbpasswd failed' >&2",
   817→       password, username]
   818→    )

1→class TailscaleManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
     4→  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
     5→  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}
     6→
     7→  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
     8→  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
     9→  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs → "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
    10→  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    11→    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    12→    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")
    13→  end
    14→
    15→  class Error < StandardError; end
    16→
    17→  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
    18→  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
    19→  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
    20→  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
    21→  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
    22→  def restore_from_state(user:)
    23→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    24→
    25→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    26→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    27→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
    28→
    29→    pull_image
    30→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    31→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    32→    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state — we rely on existing saved credentials
    33→    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) — NOT containerboot.
    34→    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    35→    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    36→    container = create_sidecar(
    37→      name: container_name,
    38→      user: user,
    39→      network: network_name,
    40→      subnet: subnet,
    41→      auth_key: nil
    42→    )
    43→    container.start
    44→
    45→    # Re-advertise routes after tailscaled reconnects — the subnet may have changed
    46→    # after a reinstall (Docker reallocates from the pool). No --reset so we preserve
    47→    # credentials and other settings in the persisted state. Also re-applies the
    48→    # canonical hostname so it reflects the current SANDCASTLE_NAME.
    49→    container.exec([
    50→      "sh", "-c",
    51→      "sleep 3 && tailscale up" \
    52→      " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
    53→      " --accept-routes" \
    54→      " --hostname=#{TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME}" \
    55→      " --timeout=60s &"
    56→    ])
    57→
    58→    user.update!(
    59→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    60→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    61→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    62→      tailscale_network: network_name,
    63→      tailscale_subnet: subnet
    64→    )
    65→    user
    66→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    67→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    68→    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
    69→  end
    70→
    71→  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
    72→  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    73→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    74→
    75→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)
    76→
    77→    user.update!(
    78→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    79→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    80→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    81→      tailscale_network: network_name
    82→    )
    83→    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    84→    user
    85→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    86→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    87→    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
    88→  end
    89→
    90→  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately.
    91→  # Called from TailscaleLoginJob — user is already in "pending" state.
    92→  def start_login(user:)
    93→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?
    94→
    95→    # If there's an old container, clean up first
    96→    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_container_id.present?
    97→
    98→    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    99→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)

    26→    def create
    27→      authorize Sandbox
    28→
    29→      manager = SandboxManager.new
    30→
    31→      # Resolve snapshot for container image
    32→      from_snapshot_name = params[:from_snapshot].presence || params[:snapshot].presence
    33→      restore_layers     = params[:restore_layers].present? ? Array(params[:restore_layers]) : nil
    34→
    35→      image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    36→        # Try to find DB record first
    37→        snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: current_user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    38→        snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{current_user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    39→      else
    40→        params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    41→      end
    42→
    43→      # Build sandbox record (use system defaults where not explicitly provided)
    44→      defaults = Setting.instance
    45→      sandbox = current_user.sandboxes.build(
    46→        name: params.require(:name),
    47→        status: "pending",
    48→        image: image,
    49→        mount_home: params.key?(:mount_home) ? params[:mount_home] : defaults.default_mount_home,
    50→        data_path: params.key?(:data_path) ? params[:data_path] : defaults.default_data_path,
    51→        tailscale: params.fetch(:tailscale) { current_user.tailscale_enabled? },
    52→        vnc_enabled: params.key?(:vnc_enabled) ? params[:vnc_enabled] : defaults.default_vnc_enabled,
    53→        vnc_geometry: params[:vnc_geometry] || "1280x900",
    54→        vnc_depth: params[:vnc_depth]&.to_i || 24,
    55→        docker_enabled: params.key?(:docker_enabled) ? params[:docker_enabled] : defaults.default_docker_enabled,
    56→        temporary: params[:temporary] || false,
    57→        smb_enabled: params[:smb_enabled] || false
    58→      )
    59→
    60→      sandbox.save!
    61→
    62→      # Restore BTRFS layers from snapshot (if requested and available)
    63→      if from_snapshot_name.present? && snap.present?
    64→        want_home = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("home")
    65→        want_data = restore_layers.nil? || restore_layers.include?("data")
    66→
    67→        if want_home && snap.home_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    68→          manager.ensure_mount_dirs(current_user, sandbox)
    69→          home_target = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/home"
    70→          BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target) rescue nil
    71→        end
    72→
    73→        if want_data && snap.data_snapshot.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    74→          data_target = if snap.data_subdir.present? && sandbox.data_path.present?
    75→            "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
    76→          elsif sandbox.data_path.present?
    77→            "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/users/#{current_user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
    78→          end
    79→          BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target) rescue nil if data_target
    80→        end
    81→      end
    82→
    83→      # Enqueue async job
    84→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    85→
    86→      # Return immediately with job_status so CLI can poll
    87→      render json: sandbox_json(sandbox), status: :created
    88→    end

   220→    def set_sandbox
   221→      @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   222→      authorize @sandbox
   223→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   224→      # Check if sandbox exists but is out of scope (destroyed or wrong user)
   225→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: params[:id])
   226→      if sandbox.nil?
   227→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} not found"
   228→      elsif sandbox.status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
   229→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} has been #{sandbox.status}"
   230→      elsif sandbox.user_id != current_user.id
   231→        raise Pundit::NotAuthorizedError, "You don't have access to this sandbox"
   232→      else
   233→        # Sandbox exists and belongs to user, but not in policy scope - this shouldn't happen
   234→        Rails.logger.error("Sandbox #{params[:id]} exists (status: #{sandbox.status}, user: #{sandbox.user_id}) but not in policy_scope for user #{current_user.id}")
   235→        raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Sandbox with ID #{params[:id]} is not accessible (status: #{sandbox.status})"
   236→      end
   237→    end

188→# Set up and start Samba for SMB file sharing (SMB3-only, no NetBIOS).
   189→# Accessible via the sandbox's Tailscale IP on port 445: smb://<ip>/home or smb://<ip>/persisted
   190→SMB_ENABLED="${SANDCASTLE_SMB_ENABLED:-0}"
   191→if command -v smbd &>/dev/null && [ "$SMB_ENABLED" = "1" ]; then
   192→    cat > /etc/samba/smb.conf << SMBCONF
   193→[global]
   194→    disable netbios = yes
   195→    smb ports = 445
   196→    server min protocol = SMB3
   197→    server max protocol = SMB3_11
   198→    smb encrypt = if_required
   199→    server signing = required
   200→    map to guest = never
   201→    restrict anonymous = 2
   202→    load printers = no
   203→    printing = bsd
   204→    printcap name = /dev/null
   205→    disable spoolss = yes
   206→    log level = 1
   207→    log file = /var/log/samba/smbd.log
   208→
   209→[home]
   210→    path = /home/$USERNAME
   211→    browseable = yes
   212→    read only = no
   213→    valid users = $USERNAME
   214→    guest ok = no
   215→    create mask = 0644
   216→    directory mask = 0755
   217→SMBCONF

   224→    path = /persisted
   225→    browseable = yes
   226→    read only = no
   227→    valid users = $USERNAME
   228→    guest ok = no
   229→    create mask = 0644
   230→    directory mask = 0755
   231→SMBCONF2
   232→    fi

   236→    touch /var/log/samba/smbd.log
   237→    smbd --foreground --no-process-group &>/var/log/samba/smbd.log &
   238→fi

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile a thorough report.

## Summary

I've conducted a thorough search of the Sandcastle codebase across all three requested areas. Here are my findings:

### 1. SMB/Samba Functionality

**Models:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/sandbox.rb` - Contains `smb_enabled` boolean field and validation that SMB requires Tailscale and an SMB password
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/user.rb` - Contains encrypted `smb_password` field with decryption error handling

**Migration:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/db/migrate/20260308000001_add_smb_to_users_and_sandboxes.rb` - Adds `smb_password` to users and `smb_enabled` to sandboxes

**Services:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb`:
  - `set_smb_password(container, user)` (line 810) - Injects SMB password into container via `smbpasswd -a -s` using docker exec
  - `update_smb_password(user:)` (line 643) - Updates password on all active SMB-enabled sandboxes
  - `container_env(user, sandbox)` (line 713) - Sets `SANDCASTLE_SMB_ENABLED` env var based on `sandbox.smb_enabled?`

**Controllers:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/smb_controller.rb` - API endpoint that calls `SandboxManager.new.update_smb_password(user: current_user)` on password change
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb` - Includes `smb_enabled` in sandbox JSON responses (line 265)

**Views:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/settings/show.html.erb` (lines 261-293) - "File Sharing (SMB)" section with password form
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/sandboxes/show.html.erb` (lines 139-159) - SMB connection info display (only if SMB enabled and Tailscale is enabled)
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb` - SMB is not shown as a pill in dashboard

**CLI:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/smb.go` - CLI command `sandcastle smb set-password` with interactive password input
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/client.go` (line 488) - `SmbSetPassword(password string)` method makes PATCH request to `/api/smb/set_password`
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go` (line 195) - `SMBEnabled` field in `CreateSandboxRequest`

**Sandbox Image:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/images/sandbox/entrypoint.sh` (lines 188-238):
  - Checks `SMB_ENABLED` env var
  - Configures Samba with SMB3-only, no NetBIOS
  - Creates two shares: `[home]` and `[persisted]` (if `/persisted` volume exists)
  - Security: disabled guest access, requires authentication, requires signing
  - Password set post-start via docker exec (not via env var)

**Requirements:**
- SMB validation requires: Tailscale enabled AND SMB password set (both checked in `Sandbox#smb_prerequisites`)
- Accessible only via Tailscale IP on port 445
- Username is the Sandcastle user's name
- Password is user's encrypted SMB password

---

### 2. Dashboard View

**Main Controller:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/dashboard_controller.rb`:
  - `index` action (line 2-7) - Renders main dashboard, fetches `@sandboxes` with associations, calls `tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)` to populate `@tailscale_ips` hash
  - `card` action (line 9-18) - AJAX endpoint for individual sandbox card refresh via Turbo
  - `stats` action (line 20-66) - Renders live CPU/MEM sparklines
  - `tailscale_ips_for(sandboxes)` (line 78-95) - Helper that fetches Docker container IPs from the user's tailscale network

**Main View:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/index.html.erb`:
  - Turbo Stream subscription to `[Current.user, "dashboard"]` for real-time updates (line 3)
  - "My Sandcastles" header with "Create Sandcastle" button
  - Renders sandbox partial with locals: `sandbox`, `vnc_active`, `tailscale_ip` (line 22)
  - Shows archived sandboxes in a collapsible section (lines 35-54)

**Sandbox Card Partial:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb`:
  - Line 1-8: Card div with conditional pulsing animation if job in progress, border color if failed
  - Lines 10-88: First row with status indicator, sandbox name, feature pills (TEMP, home, data, Tailscale with IP, VNC, routes), and action buttons
  - Lines 89-154: Action buttons (Start/Terminal/VNC/Snapshot/Stop/Destroy or Retry if failed)
  - Lines 172-208: Container info (ID, image, creation/running time) and live CPU/MEM stats via Turbo Frame

**Feature Pills:**
- TEMP pill (amber) - if `sandbox.temporary?`
- home pill (blue) - if `sandbox.mount_home?`
- data pill (blue) - if `sandbox.data_path.present?`
- Tailscale pill (purple) - if `sandbox.tailscale?`, includes IP if available: `Tailscale · <ip>`
- VNC pill (indigo) - if VNC is active
- Routes pills (green) - clickable links for each route

**Broadcast Updates:**
- Sandbox broadcasts prepend/replace/remove to dashboard stream on create/update/destroy (Sandbox model lines 78-107)

---

### 3. Tailscale IP Access/Display

**Manager Service:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/tailscale_manager.rb`:
  - `sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)` (line 283) - **Main method to get sandbox IP**
    - Returns nil unless: sandbox has tailscale enabled, container_id present, user has tailscale_network
    - Fetches from: `container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")`
    - Returns the sandbox's IP on the user's tailscale bridge network (e.g., `10.89.42.5`)

**Controller Usage:**
- `DashboardController#index` (line 6) - Calls `tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)` which returns hash of `{sandbox_id => ip}`
- `DashboardController#card` (line 13) - Fetches single sandbox Tailscale IP for card refresh
- `Api::SandboxesController#sandbox_json` (lines 275-277) - Includes `tailscale_ip` in JSON if `sandbox.tailscale?`

**Data Flow:**
1. Dashboard fetches all sandboxes
2. For each sandbox where `tailscale?` is true and `container_id` is present:
   - Get Docker container
   - Look up user's `tailscale_network` (bridge network name like `sc-ts-net-{username}`)
   - Extract IP from NetworkSettings
3. Passed to view as `tailscale_ip` local variable

**Display Locations:**
- **Dashboard card** (line 71): Purple Tailscale pill shows `Tailscale · <ip>` if IP available
- **Sandbox show page**: Not explicitly shown in view template, but available for SMB connection instructions (references `<tailscale-ip>` placeholder)
- **API responses**: JSON includes `tailscale_ip` field in Sandbox type
- **Guide page**: Documents usage of Tailscale IP for SMB mounting

**Example Flow:**
1. User creates sandbox with `--tailscale`
2. TailscaleManager creates bridge network `sc-ts-net-{username}` with subnet (e.g., `10.89.42.0/24`)
3. Sandbox container connects to this network
4. Container gets IP like `10.89.42.5`
5. Dashboard fetches this via Docker API using network name from user's `tailscale_network` field
6. Displayed as `Tailscale · 10.89.42.5` in dashboard pill
7. Used in SMB mount instructions: `smb://10.89.42.5/home`

**API Types:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`:
  - `Sandbox` struct (line 16): `TailscaleIP string` field
  - `ConnectInfo` struct (line 43): `TailscaleIP string` field (returned by `/api/sandboxes/{id}/connect`)
  - `TailscaleLoginStatus` struct (line 228): `TailscaleIP string` field
  - `TailscaleStatus` struct (line 239): `TailscaleIP string` field for sidecar status

file.content:
class DashboardController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)
    @archived_sandboxes = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.includes(:routes).order(:name)
    @vnc_active_ids = running_vnc_ids
    @tailscale_ips = tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)
  end

  def card
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox
    vnc_active = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    tailscale_ip = sandbox.tailscale? ? TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox) : nil
    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(helpers.dom_id(sandbox), partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: sandbox, vnc_active: vnc_active, tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip })
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    # Sandbox was destroyed/archived while the dashboard was open — remove the card
    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove("sandbox_#{params[:id]}")
  end

  def stats
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :stats?

    if sandbox.status == "running" && sandbox.container_id.present?
      @stats = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        raw = container.stats(stream: false)

        next nil if raw.blank?

        networks = raw["networks"] || {}
        net_rx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["rx_bytes"] || 0 }
        net_tx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["tx_bytes"] || 0 }

        blkio = raw.dig("blkio_stats", "io_service_bytes_recursive") || []
        disk_read = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "read" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }
        disk_write = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "write" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }

        {
          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          net_rx: net_rx,
          net_tx: net_tx,
          disk_read: disk_read,
          disk_write: disk_write,
          pids: raw.dig("pids_stats", "current") || 0
        }
      end
    end

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: @stats, sandbox: sandbox } }
      format.json { render json: @stats ? { cpu: @stats[:cpu_percent], mem: @stats[:memory_mb].round(1) } : { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: nil } }
      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: sandbox } }
      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  end

  private

  def running_vnc_ids
    Dir.glob(File.join(VncManager::DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-*.yml")).filter_map { |f|
      File.basename(f).match(/\Avnc-(\d+)\.yml\z/)&.[](1)&.to_i
    }.to_set
  rescue
    Set.new
  end

  def tailscale_ips_for(sandboxes)
    ts_sandboxes = sandboxes.select { |s| s.tailscale? && s.container_id.present? }
    return {} if ts_sandboxes.empty?

    all_containers = Docker::Container.all
    container_map = all_containers.each_with_object({}) { |c, h| h[c.id] = c }

    ts_sandboxes.each_with_object({}) do |sandbox, ips|
      container = container_map[sandbox.container_id]
      next unless container
      network = sandbox.user.tailscale_network
      next unless network.present?
      ip = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", network, "IPAddress")
      ips[sandbox.id] = ip if ip.present?
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    {}
  end
end

content.text:
Added an SMB pill to the dashboard. It appears as an orange "smb" link after the VNC pill when:
- `sandbox.smb_enabled?` is true
- A tailscale IP is available

The link uses the format `smb://username:password@tailscale-ip/home`, pulling the username from `sandbox.user.name` and the password from `sandbox.user.smb_password`. Clicking it will open the SMB share in the OS file manager (Finder on macOS, Explorer on Windows).
Let me check the current state first.

To show the actual version instead of `:latest`, I need to resolve the Docker image's actual tag/version. Let me check how the image is set and if there's version info available from the container.

content.text: The sandbox stores `image_id` (the Docker SHA) and `image_built_at`, but not the resolved version tag. The `image` field stores the reference like `sandcastle-sandbox:latest`. I need to check what labels are available on the image that might contain a version.

file.content:
# Thin layer on top of the pre-built base image.
# Changes here (entrypoint, config, scripts) build in seconds.
# To rebuild the base: trigger the "Rebuild Sandbox Base Image" workflow
# or change files in images/sandbox/base/.
FROM ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox-base:latest

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

# Always pull latest security patches and package updates
RUN apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# SSH agent forwarding: profile.d sets SSH_AUTH_SOCK to the stable
# symlink maintained by sc-tmux, so tmux shells always find the agent.
RUN printf '[ -S "$HOME/.ssh/agent_sock" ] && export SSH_AUTH_SOCK="$HOME/.ssh/agent_sock"\n' \
       > /etc/profile.d/ssh-agent.sh

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
COPY docker-restart.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart
COPY sc-tmux.sh /usr/local/bin/sc-tmux
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart /usr/local/bin/sc-tmux

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -sf /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

EXPOSE 22 5900 6080 7681 7682
EXPOSE 60000-61000/udp

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

content.text:
The version is baked into the image as `/etc/sandcastle-version` and also available via `SANDCASTLE_VERSION` build arg. The `fetch_image_info` method already inspects labels/env but doesn't extract the version. The simplest approach: also extract the version from the image info and store it, or resolve it from the container's env/labels.

Let me check if there's a `SANDCASTLE_VERSION` env var or label on the image.

content.text: The version is only a build ARG (not ENV), so it won't be in the container's env vars. It's written to `/etc/sandcastle-version` file. Let me check the release/build workflow to see if a version label is set.

In release builds, `SANDCASTLE_VERSION` is set to the git tag (e.g. `v0.8.75`). This is written to `/etc/sandcastle-version` inside the image. 

The best approach: extract the version from the running container via `docker exec cat /etc/sandcastle-version`. But that's expensive for every dashboard load. Better: store it in `fetch_image_info` by reading it from the image's filesystem.

Actually, the simplest approach: the `image` field on sandbox already stores the image ref. In production, the release workflow tags images with version tags. But currently it seems to store `sandcastle-sandbox:latest`.

Let me check what image ref is actually stored in the DB.

Actually, the simplest and most robust way: add the version as a label in the Dockerfile, and read it in `fetch_image_info`. Or, since `fetch_image_info` already reads labels, I can add `org.opencontainers.image.version` to the Dockerfile.

file.content:
class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :container_metrics, dependent: :delete_all

  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) } }
  validates :name, format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" },
    unless: -> { status.in?(%w[destroyed archived]) }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed archived] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }
  validate :smb_prerequisites, if: -> { smb_enabled? }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) }
  scope :archived, -> { where(status: "archived") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command
    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

  def smb_enabled?
    smb_enabled
  end

  # Job lifecycle management
  def job_in_progress?
    job_status.present?
  end

  def job_failed?
    job_error.present?
  end

  def start_job(status)
    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
  end

  def finish_job
    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  def fail_job(error_message)
    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  private

  def smb_prerequisites
    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires Tailscale to be enabled") unless user&.tailscale_enabled?
    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires an SMB password to be set in Settings") unless user&.smb_password.present?
  end

  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    broadcast_prepend_to(
      [ user, "dashboard" ],
      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
      locals: { sandbox: self },
      target: "sandboxes"
    )
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_prepend: #{e.message}")
  end

  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    # If sandbox is destroyed or archived, remove it from the active dashboard
    if status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
      broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
    else
      broadcast_replace_to(
        [ user, "dashboard" ],
        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
        locals: { sandbox: self },
        target: dom_id(self)
      )
    end
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_replace: #{e.message}")
  end

  def broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_remove: #{e.message}")
  end
end

There's no `image_version` column. The best approach: 

1. Add an `org.opencontainers.image.version` label to the Dockerfile
2. Store the version in a new `image_version` column on sandboxes
3. Read it in `fetch_image_info` and display it in the dashboard

Actually, let me think simpler. The version is already baked into `/etc/sandcastle-version` via the build arg. I can add it as a LABEL too, and then read it in `fetch_image_info`. Let me:

1. Add `LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version="${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}"` to the Dockerfile
2. Add `image_version` column to sandboxes
3. Extract it in `fetch_image_info`
4. Display it in the dashboard instead of the image name

# Thin layer on top of the pre-built base image.
# Changes here (entrypoint, config, scripts) build in seconds.
# To rebuild the base: trigger the "Rebuild Sandbox Base Image" workflow
# or change files in images/sandbox/base/.
FROM ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox-base:latest

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

# Always pull latest security patches and package updates
RUN apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# SSH agent forwarding: profile.d sets SSH_AUTH_SOCK to the stable
# symlink maintained by sc-tmux, so tmux shells always find the agent.
RUN printf '[ -S "$HOME/.ssh/agent_sock" ] && export SSH_AUTH_SOCK="$HOME/.ssh/agent_sock"\n' \
       > /etc/profile.d/ssh-agent.sh

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
COPY docker-restart.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart
COPY sc-tmux.sh /usr/local/bin/sc-tmux
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart /usr/local/bin/sc-tmux

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -sf /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

EXPOSE 22 5900 6080 7681 7682
EXPOSE 60000-61000/udp

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

683→  # Extracts the image SHA and BUILD_DATE from a local Docker image.
   684→  # Returns a hash with :image_id and :image_built_at (or nils on error).
   685→  def fetch_image_info(image_ref)
   686→    img    = Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   687→    config = img.json.dig("Config") || {}
   688→    labels = config["Labels"] || {}
   689→
   690→    date_str = labels["org.opencontainers.image.created"] || labels["BUILD_DATE"]
   691→    if date_str.nil?
   692→      env_entry = (config["Env"] || []).find { |e| e.start_with?("BUILD_DATE=") }
   693→      date_str  = env_entry&.split("=", 2)&.last
   694→    end
   695→
   696→    {
   697→      image_id:       img.id,
   698→      image_built_at: date_str ? Time.zone.parse(date_str) : nil
   699→    }
   700→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ArgumentError, TypeError
   701→    { image_id: nil, image_built_at: nil }
   702→  end
