    86→    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
    87→      container.stop rescue nil
    88→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    89→      sandbox.update!(container_id: nil, status: "pending")
    90→      raise Error, "Failed to attach tenant network: #{e.message}"
    91→    end
    92→
    93→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    94→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    95→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    96→
    97→    # Set SMB password via exec (avoids leaking password in container env/metadata)
    98→    set_smb_password(container, sandbox.user) if sandbox.smb_enabled?
    99→  end
   100→
   101→  # Public method for job usage
   102→  def ensure_image(image)
   103→    Docker::Image.get(image)
   104→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   105→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
   106→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
   107→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   108→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
   109→  end
   110→
   111→  # Public method for job usage
   112→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   113→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   114→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   115→
   116→    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   117→    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   118→
   119→    if sandbox.mount_home
   120→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   121→      ensure_dir(dir)
   122→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   123→    end
   124→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   125→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   126→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   127→
   128→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   129→      ensure_dir(dir)
   130→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   131→    end
   132→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   133→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   134→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   135→      ensure_dir(dir)
   136→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   137→    end
   138→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   139→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   140→  end
   141→
   142→  def destroy(sandbox:, archive: false)
   143→    begin
   144→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   145→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   146→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   147→    end
   148→
   149→    begin
   150→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   151→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   152→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   153→    end
   154→
   155→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   156→
   157→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   158→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   159→    end
   160→
   161→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   162→      begin
   163→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   164→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   165→        container.delete(force: true)
   166→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   167→        # Container already gone
   168→      end
   169→    end
   170→
   171→    if archive
   172→      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
   173→      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
   174→      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
   175→      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
   176→    else
   177→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   178→    end
   179→
   180→    # Remove per-user network if this was the last active sandbox
   181→    begin
   182→      NetworkManager.new.cleanup_user_network(sandbox.user)
   183→    rescue NetworkManager::Error => e
   184→      Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager#destroy: network cleanup failed for #{sandbox.user.name}: #{e.message}")
   185→    end
   186→  end
   187→
   188→  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   189→  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   190→  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   191→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   192→
   193→    user = sandbox.user
   194→
   195→    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   196→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   197→
   198→    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   199→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   200→
   201→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   202→    # Strip the timestamp prefix added during archival (e.g. "20260307123456-mybox" → "mybox")
   203→    original_name = sandbox.name.sub(/\A\d{14}-/, "")
   204→    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil, name: original_name)
   205→
   206→    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   207→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   208→    end
   209→
   210→    sandbox
   211→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   212→    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   213→  end
   214→
   215→  def start(sandbox:)
   216→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   217→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   218→
   219→    user = sandbox.user
   220→
   221→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   222→      begin
   223→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   224→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   225→        old.delete(force: true)
   226→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   227→        # already gone
   228→      end
   229→    end
   230→
   231→    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   232→    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
   233→    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
   234→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   235→
   236→    # Refresh image info in case the image was updated since sandbox creation
   237→    info = fetch_image_info(sandbox.image)
   238→    sandbox.update!(image_id: info[:image_id], image_built_at: info[:image_built_at], image_version: info[:image_version])
   239→
   240→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   241→
   242→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   243→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   244→
   245→    sandbox
   246→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   247→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   248→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   249→  end
   250→
   251→  def stop(sandbox:)
   252→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   253→
   254→    begin
   255→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   256→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   257→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   258→    end
   259→
   260→    begin
   261→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   262→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   263→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   264→    end
   265→
   266→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   267→
   268→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→      begin
   270→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   271→        container.stop(t: 10)
   272→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   273→        # already gone
   274→      end
   275→    end
   276→
   277→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   278→    sandbox
   279→  end
   280→
   281→  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze
   282→
   283→  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
   284→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   285→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   286→
   287→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288→    user = sandbox.user.name
   289→
   290→    case service
   291→    when "docker"
   292→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
   293→    when "vnc"
   294→      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
   295→      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
   296→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
   297→    end
   298→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   299→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   300→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   301→    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
   302→  end
   303→
   304→  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
   305→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   306→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   307→
   308→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   309→
   310→    case service
   311→    when "docker"
   312→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   313→    when "vnc"
   314→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   315→    end
   316→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   317→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   318→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   319→    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
   320→  end
   321→
   322→  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   323→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   324→
   325→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   326→    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   327→      .force_encoding("UTF-8").encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace, replace: "")
   328→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   329→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   330→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   331→    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   332→  end
   333→
   334→  def status(sandbox:)
   335→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   336→
   337→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   338→    info = container.json
   339→    {
   340→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   341→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   342→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   343→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   344→    }
   345→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   346→    { state: "not_found" }
   347→  end
   348→
   349→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   350→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   351→  #
   352→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data"
   353→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   354→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   355→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   356→
   357→    user = sandbox.user
   358→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   359→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data]
   360→
   361→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   362→      user: user,
   363→      name: name,
   364→      label: label,
   365→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   366→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   367→    )
   368→
   369→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   370→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   371→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   372→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   373→      image = container.commit(
   374→        repo: repo,
   375→        tag: name,
   376→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   377→      )
   378→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   379→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   380→    end
   381→
   382→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   383→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   384→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   385→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   386→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   387→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   388→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   389→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   390→      end
   391→    end
   392→
   393→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   394→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   395→      if data_subdir.present?
   396→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   397→      else
   398→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   399→      end
   400→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   401→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   402→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   403→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   404→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   405→      end
   406→    end
   407→
   408→    snap.save!
   409→    snap
   410→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   411→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   412→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   413→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   414→  end
   415→
   416→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   417→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   418→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   419→    {
   420→      name: snap.name,
   421→      image: snap.docker_image,
   422→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   423→      created_at: snap.created_at
   424→    }
   425→  end
   426→
   427→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   428→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   429→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   430→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   431→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   432→  end
   433→
   434→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   435→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   436→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   437→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   438→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   439→  end
   440→
   441→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   442→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   443→
   444→    if snap
   445→      # Remove Docker image
   446→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   447→        begin
   448→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   449→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   450→          # Already gone
   451→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   452→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   453→        end
   454→      end
   455→
   456→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   457→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   458→        begin
   459→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   460→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   461→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   462→        end
   463→      end
   464→
   465→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   466→        begin
   467→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   468→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   469→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   470→        end
   471→      end
   472→
   473→      snap.destroy!
   474→    else
   475→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   476→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   477→      begin
   478→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   479→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   480→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   481→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   482→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   483→      end
   484→    end
   485→  end
   486→
   487→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   488→    user = sandbox.user
   489→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   490→
   491→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   492→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   493→
   494→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   495→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   496→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   497→    else
   498→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   499→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   500→    end
   501→
   502→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   503→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   504→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   505→
   506→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   507→    if restore_container
   508→      begin
   509→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   510→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   511→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   512→      end
   513→    end
   514→
   515→    begin
   516→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   517→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   518→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   519→    end
   520→
   521→    begin
   522→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   523→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   524→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   525→    end
   526→
   527→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   528→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   529→    end
   530→
   531→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   532→      begin
   533→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   534→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   535→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   536→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   537→        # Already gone
   538→      end
   539→    end
   540→
   541→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   542→    if restore_home
   543→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   544→      begin
   545→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   546→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   547→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   548→      end
   549→    end
   550→
   551→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   552→    if restore_data
   553→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   554→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   555→      else
   556→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   557→      end
   558→      begin
   559→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   560→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   561→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   562→      end
   563→    end
   564→
   565→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   566→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   567→    # Clear stale container reference before recreating — prevents the record
   568→    # from pointing at a dead container if create_container_and_start fails.
   569→    sandbox.update!(image: final_image, container_id: nil, status: "pending")
   570→
   571→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   572→
   573→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   574→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   575→    end
   576→
   577→    sandbox
   578→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   579→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   580→  end
   581→
   582→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   583→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   584→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   585→
   586→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   587→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   588→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   589→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   590→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   591→        next if tag_name.blank?
   592→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   593→
   594→        Snapshot.create!(
   595→          user: user,
   596→          name: tag_name,
   597→          docker_image: tag,
   598→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   599→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   600→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   601→        )
   602→      end
   603→    end
   604→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   605→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   606→  end
   607→
   608→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   609→    {
   610→      name: snap.name,
   611→      label: snap.label,
   612→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   613→      layers: snap.layers,
   614→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   615→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   616→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   617→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   618→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   619→      created_at: snap.created_at
   620→    }
   621→  end
   622→
   623→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   624→    user = sandbox.user.name
   625→
   626→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   627→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   628→    end
   629→
   630→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   631→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   632→
   633→    {
   634→      host: ts_ip,
   635→      port: 22,
   636→      user: user,
   637→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   638→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   639→    }
   640→  end
   641→
   642→  # Update the SMB password on all active SMB-enabled sandboxes for a user.
   643→  def update_smb_password(user:)
   644→    raise Error, "No SMB password set" unless user.smb_password.present?
   645→
   646→    user.sandboxes.active.where(smb_enabled: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   647→      next if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   648→
   649→      begin
   650→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   651→        set_smb_password(container, user)
   652→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   653→        # Container gone, skip
   654→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   655→        Rails.logger.warn("Failed to update SMB password for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
   656→      end
   657→    end
   658→  end
   659→
   660→  private
   661→
   662→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   663→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   664→    max_attempts.times do
   665→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   666→
   667→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   668→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   669→        sleep delay
   670→        next
   671→      end
   672→
   673→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   674→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   675→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   676→
   677→      sleep delay
   678→    end
   679→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   680→    nil
   681→  end
   682→
   683→  # Extracts the image SHA and BUILD_DATE from a local Docker image.
   684→  # Returns a hash with :image_id and :image_built_at (or nils on error).
   685→  def fetch_image_info(image_ref)
   686→    img    = Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   687→    config = img.json.dig("Config") || {}
   688→    labels = config["Labels"] || {}
   689→
   690→    date_str = labels["org.opencontainers.image.created"] || labels["BUILD_DATE"]
   691→    if date_str.nil?
   692→      env_entry = (config["Env"] || []).find { |e| e.start_with?("BUILD_DATE=") }
   693→      date_str  = env_entry&.split("=", 2)&.last
   694→    end
   695→
   696→    {
   697→      image_id:       img.id,
   698→      image_built_at: date_str ? Time.zone.parse(date_str) : nil,
   699→      image_version:  labels["org.opencontainers.image.version"]
   700→    }
   701→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ArgumentError, TypeError
   702→    { image_id: nil, image_built_at: nil, image_version: nil }
   703→  end
   704→
   705→  def connect_to_network(container)
   706→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   707→    network.connect(container.id)
   708→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   709→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   710→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   711→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   712→  end
   713→
   714→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   715→    env = [
   716→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   717→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   718→    ]
   719→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   720→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   721→    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
   722→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   723→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   724→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   725→    env << "SANDCASTLE_DOCKER_ENABLED=#{sandbox.docker_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   726→    env << "SANDCASTLE_SMB_ENABLED=#{sandbox.smb_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   727→    env << "SANDCASTLE_HOME_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.mount_home ? '1' : '0'}"
   728→    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PERSISTED=#{sandbox.data_path.present? ? '1' : '0'}"
   729→    env << "SANDCASTLE_DATA_PATH=#{sandbox.data_path}" if sandbox.data_path.present?
   730→    env
   731→  end
   732→
   733→  def container_runtime
   734→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   735→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   736→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   737→        "sysbox-runc"
   738→      else
   739→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   740→        "runc"
   741→      end
   742→    end
   743→  end
   744→
   745→  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
   746→  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
   747→  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
   748→  def ensure_dir(path)
   749→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   750→  rescue Errno::EACCES
   751→    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
   752→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   753→  end
   754→
   755→  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
   756→  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
   757→  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   758→    stat = File.stat(path)
   759→    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   760→    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
   761→    docker_chmod(path, "777")
   762→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   763→    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
   764→  end
   765→
   766→  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via a busybox container.
   767→  def docker_chown(path)
   768→    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
   769→  end
   770→
   771→  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
   772→    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
   773→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   774→    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
   775→  end
   776→
   777→  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
   778→    image = fix_image
   779→    c = Docker::Container.create(
   780→      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
   781→      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
   782→    )
   783→    c.start
   784→    result = c.wait(30)
   785→    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
   786→    unless exit_code == 0
   787→      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
   788→    end
   789→  ensure
   790→    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   791→  end
   792→
   793→  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
   794→  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
   795→  def fix_image
   796→    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
   797→      begin
   798→        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
   799→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   800→        next
   801→      end
   802→    end
   803→    # Last resort: use any image already present
   804→    all = Docker::Image.all
   805→    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
   806→    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
   807→    tags&.first || all.first.id
   808→  end
   809→
   810→  # Inject SMB password into the container via exec, avoiding env var leakage.
   811→  def set_smb_password(container, user)
   812→    return unless user.smb_password.present?
   813→
   814→    username = user.name
   815→    password = user.smb_password
   816→    container.exec(
   817→      ["bash", "-c", "printf '%s\\n%s\\n' \"$0\" \"$0\" | smbpasswd -a -s \"$1\" 2>&1 || echo 'Warning: smbpasswd failed' >&2",
   818→       password, username]
   819→    )
   820→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   821→    Rails.logger.warn("set_smb_password failed for #{user.name}: #{e.message}")
   822→  end
   823→
   824→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   825→    binds = []
   826→    if sandbox.mount_home
   827→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   828→    end
   829→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   830→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   831→      binds << "#{host_path}:/persisted"
   832→    end
   833→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   834→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   835→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   836→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   837→    end
   838→    binds
   839→  end
   840→end
   841→

    17→
    18→      snap = SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(

    23→        data_subdir: params[:data_subdir]
    24→      )
    25→
    26→      render json: SandboxManager.new.snapshot_json(snap), status: :created
    27→    end

    31→    end

    35→      render json: { status: "deleted" }

    40→    def set_snapshot
    41→      @snapshot = policy_scope(Snapshot).find_by!(name: params[:name])
    42→      authorize @snapshot
    43→    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    44→      # Try importing legacy Docker snapshots first
    45→      SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(current_user)

    48→    end
    49→  end

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}
    32→
    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→install options:
    66→  --from-backup <file>   Restore data from a backup file after installing.
    67→                         Secrets, database, and user data are loaded from the
    68→                         backup instead of being generated fresh.
    69→                         SANDCASTLE_HOST is still required in sandcastle.env.
    70→                         SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD are not required.
    71→
    72→Environment:
    73→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    74→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    75→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    76→
    77→Workflow (fresh install):
    78→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    79→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    80→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    81→
    82→Workflow (restore from backup):
    83→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config (set SANDCASTLE_HOST)
    84→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # set host, TLS mode — no admin password needed
    85→  3. sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
    86→USAGE
    87→  exit 0
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    91→
    92→load_env() {
    93→  local script_dir
    94→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    95→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    96→
    97→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    98→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    99→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
   100→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
   101→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
   102→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
   103→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   104→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
   105→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   106→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
   110→    set -a
   111→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   112→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   113→    set +a
   114→    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   115→  fi
   116→}
   117→
   118→# ═══ derive_vars ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   119→
   120→derive_vars() {
   121→  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
   122→  APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
   123→  SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
   124→  SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
   125→  SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$SANDCASTLE_USER}"
   126→  SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
   127→  SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
   128→  SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
   129→  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   130→  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
   131→  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
   132→  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   133→  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"
   134→
   135→  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME/dockyard}"
   136→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   137→
   138→  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   139→  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
   140→  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
   141→  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
   142→  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
   143→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
   144→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
   145→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
   146→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   147→
   148→  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/run/docker.sock"
   149→  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/bin/docker"
   150→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}

    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→install options:
    66→  --from-backup <file>   Restore data from a backup file after installing.
    67→                         Secrets, database, and user data are loaded from the
    68→                         backup instead of being generated fresh.
    69→                         SANDCASTLE_HOST is still required in sandcastle.env.
    70→                         SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD are not required.
    71→
    72→Environment:
    73→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    74→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    75→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    76→
    77→Workflow (fresh install):
    78→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    79→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    80→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    81→
    82→Workflow (restore from backup):
    83→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config (set SANDCASTLE_HOST)
    84→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # set host, TLS mode — no admin password needed
    85→  3. sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
    86→USAGE
    87→  exit 0
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    91→
    92→load_env() {
    93→  local script_dir
    94→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    95→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    96→
    97→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    98→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    99→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
   100→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
   101→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
   102→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
   103→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   104→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
   105→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   106→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
   110→    set -a
   111→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   112→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   113→    set +a
   114→    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   115→  fi
   116→}
   117→

   119→
   120→derive_vars() {
   121→  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"

   129→  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   130→  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
   131→  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
   132→  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   133→  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"
   134→
   135→  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME/dockyard}"
   136→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   137→
   138→  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   139→  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
   140→  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
   141→  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
   142→  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
   143→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
   144→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
   145→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
   146→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   147→
   148→  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/run/docker.sock"
   149→  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/bin/docker"
   150→
   151→  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo "amd64")
   152→
   153→  # Resolve password from file if needed
   154→  if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE:-}" ]; then
   155→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE" ] && die "Password file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE"
   156→    SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="$(cat "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE")"
   157→  fi
   158→}

   161→# Copy deploying user's SSH keys to sandcastle user
   162→
   163→setup_ssh_keys() {
   164→  local deploying_user="${SUDO_USER:-}"
   165→
   166→  if [ -z "$deploying_user" ]; then
   167→    warn "No SUDO_USER found — skipping SSH key setup"
   168→    return
   169→  fi
   170→
   171→  local source_keys="/home/${deploying_user}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
   172→  local target_dir="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.ssh"
   173→  local target_keys="${target_dir}/authorized_keys"
   174→
   175→  if [ ! -f "$source_keys" ]; then
   176→    warn "No SSH keys found for user '${deploying_user}' — skipping SSH key setup"
   177→    return
   178→  fi
   179→
   180→  info "Setting up SSH keys for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."
   181→
   182→  # Create .ssh directory if it doesn't exist
   183→  mkdir -p "$target_dir"
   184→  chmod 700 "$target_dir"
   185→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_dir"
   186→
   187→  # Copy authorized_keys
   188→  cp "$source_keys" "$target_keys"
   189→  chmod 600 "$target_keys"
   190→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_keys"
   191→
   192→  ok "SSH keys configured for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   193→}

1→class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
     2→  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts
     3→
     4→  belongs_to :user
     5→  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
     6→  has_many :container_metrics, dependent: :delete_all
     7→
     8→  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
     9→  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze
    10→
    11→  validates :name, presence: true,
    12→    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) } }
    13→  validates :name, format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" },
    14→    unless: -> { status.in?(%w[destroyed archived]) }
    15→  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed archived] }
    16→  validates :image, presence: true
    17→  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
    18→  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }
    19→  validate :smb_prerequisites, if: -> { smb_enabled? }
    20→
    21→  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) }
    22→  scope :archived, -> { where(status: "archived") }
    23→  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }
    24→
    25→  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
    26→  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    27→  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    28→  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    29→
    30→  def full_name
    31→    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def connect_command
    35→    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  def routed?
    39→    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  def temp?
    43→    temporary?
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  def smb_enabled?
    47→    smb_enabled
    48→  end
    49→
    50→  # Job lifecycle management
    51→  def job_in_progress?
    52→    job_status.present?
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  def job_failed?
    56→    job_error.present?
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def start_job(status)
    60→    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
    61→  end
    62→
    63→  def finish_job
    64→    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
    65→  end
    66→
    67→  def fail_job(error_message)
    68→    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
    69→  end
    70→
    71→  private
    72→
    73→  def smb_prerequisites
    74→    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires Tailscale to be enabled") unless user&.tailscale_enabled?
    75→    errors.add(:smb_enabled, "requires an SMB password to be set in Settings") unless user&.smb_password.present?
    76→  end
    77→
    78→  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    79→    broadcast_prepend_to(
    80→      [ user, "dashboard" ],
    81→      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
    82→      locals: { sandbox: self },
    83→      target: "sandboxes"
    84→    )
    85→  rescue => e
    86→    Rails.logger.warn("Sandbox#broadcast_prepend: #{e.message}")
    87→  end
    88→
    89→  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    90→    # If sandbox is destroyed or archived, remove it from the active dashboard
    91→    if status.in?(%w[destroyed archived])
    92→      broadcast_remove_to([ user, "dashboard" ], target: dom_id(self))
    93→    else
    94→      broadcast_replace_to(
    95→        [ user, "dashboard" ],
    96→        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
    97→        locals: { sandbox: self },
    98→        target: dom_id(self)
    99→      )
   100→    end

class Setting < ApplicationRecord
  encrypts :github_client_secret, :google_client_secret, :smtp_password

  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
  validates :default_vnc_enabled, inclusion: { in: [ true, false ] }
  validates :default_mount_home, inclusion: { in: [ true, false ] }
  validates :default_docker_enabled, inclusion: { in: [ true, false ] }

  def self.instance
    find_or_create_by(id: 1)
  end

  # --- Class-level accessors with ENV fallback ---

  %i[github_client_id google_client_id].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret].each do |attr|
    define_singleton_method(attr) do
      val = instance.send(attr)
      val.presence || ENV[attr.to_s.upcase]
    end
  end

  def self.github_configured?
    github_client_id.present? && github_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.google_configured?
    google_client_id.present? && google_client_secret.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_configured?
    instance.smtp_address.present?
  end

  def self.smtp_from_address
    val = instance.smtp_from_address
    val.presence || ENV["SMTP_FROM_ADDRESS"] || "noreply@example.com"
  end

  # Return nil instead of raising on unreadable encrypted values (e.g. after key rotation)
  %i[github_client_secret google_client_secret smtp_password].each do |attr|
    define_method(attr) do
      super()
    rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
      nil

1→class SandboxPurgeJob < ApplicationJob
     2→  queue_as :default
     3→
     4→  def perform
     5→    # Find all archived sandboxes. Group by user so we can apply per-user retention windows.
     6→    Sandbox.where(status: "archived").includes(:user).find_each do |sandbox|
     7→      next unless sandbox.archived_at.present?
     8→
     9→      retention = sandbox.user.effective_archive_retention_days
    10→      next if retention <= 0 # 0 means disable archiving (but sandbox was archived before setting changed)
    11→
    12→      if sandbox.archived_at < retention.days.ago
    13→        sandbox.with_lock do
    14→          sandbox.reload
    15→          next unless sandbox.status == "archived" && sandbox.archived_at.present?
    16→
    17→          recomputed_retention = sandbox.user.effective_archive_retention_days
    18→          next if recomputed_retention <= 0
    19→          next unless sandbox.archived_at < recomputed_retention.days.ago
    20→
    21→          Rails.logger.info("SandboxPurgeJob: purging #{sandbox.full_name} (archived #{sandbox.archived_at}, retention #{recomputed_retention}d)")
    22→          SandboxManager.new.destroy(sandbox: sandbox, archive: false)
    23→        end
    24→      end
    25→    rescue => e
    26→      Rails.logger.error("SandboxPurgeJob: failed to purge sandbox #{sandbox.id}: #{e.message}")
    27→    end
    28→  end
    29→end
    30→

1→class SandboxRestoreJob < ApplicationJob
     2→  queue_as :default
     3→
     4→  def perform(sandbox_id:)
     5→    sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id)
     6→    return unless sandbox
     7→    return unless sandbox.status == "archived" # Idempotent
     8→
     9→    begin
    10→      SandboxManager.new.restore_from_archive(sandbox: sandbox)
    11→      sandbox.finish_job
    12→    rescue => e
    13→      Rails.logger.error("SandboxRestoreJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    14→      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to restore: #{e.message}")
    15→      raise
    16→    end
    17→  end
    18→end
    19→

# ═══ cmd_backup ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cmd_backup() {
  local output=""
  local skip_sandbox_volumes=false
  local skip_snapshot_images=false

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --output|-o)
        output="$2"
        shift 2
        ;;
      --no-sandbox-volumes)
        skip_sandbox_volumes=true
        shift
        ;;
      --no-snapshot-images)
        skip_snapshot_images=true
        shift
        ;;
      *)
        die "Unknown option: $1 (run 'sandcastle-admin help' for usage)"
        ;;
    esac
  done

  command -v zstd >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "zstd not found — install: apt-get install zstd"
  command -v rsync >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "rsync not found — install: apt-get install rsync"

  # Get app version from the running web container (fall back gracefully)
  local version
  version=$($DOCKER compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T web \
    ./bin/rails runner "puts Sandcastle::VERSION rescue puts '0.0.0'" 2>/dev/null \
    | tail -1 || echo "unknown")
  version="${version:-unknown}"

  local timestamp
  timestamp=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S")
  local default_output="./sandcastle-backup-${timestamp}-${version}.tar.zst"
  output="${output:-$default_output}"

  local work_dir
  work_dir=$(mktemp -d)
  trap "rm -rf '$work_dir'" EXIT

  local backup_dir="$work_dir/sandcastle-backup"
  mkdir -p "$backup_dir"/{db,secrets,data}

  echo ""
  echo -e "${BLUE}═══ Sandcastle Backup ═══${NC}"
  echo ""
  info "Output: $output"
  echo ""

  # ── Dump PostgreSQL databases ─────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Dumping PostgreSQL databases..."
  local db
  for db in sandcastle_production sandcastle_production_cache sandcastle_production_queue sandcastle_production_cable; do
    info "  pg_dump $db..."
    $DOCKER compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" exec -T postgres \
      pg_dump -U sandcastle --format=custom "$db" \
      > "$backup_dir/db/${db}.pgdump"
    ok "  ${db}.pgdump"
  done

  # ── Secrets ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Backing up secrets..."
  local rails_secrets="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
  local postgres_secrets="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres/.secrets"

  if [ -f "$rails_secrets" ]; then
    cp "$rails_secrets" "$backup_dir/secrets/rails.secrets"
    ok "  rails.secrets"
  else
    warn "  Rails secrets not found at $rails_secrets"
  fi

  if [ -f "$postgres_secrets" ]; then
    cp "$postgres_secrets" "$backup_dir/secrets/postgres.secrets"
    ok "  postgres.secrets"
  else
    warn "  Postgres secrets not found at $postgres_secrets"
  fi

  # Let's Encrypt cert (nice-to-have)
  local acme_json="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/acme.json"
  if [ -f "$acme_json" ] && [ -s "$acme_json" ]; then
    cp "$acme_json" "$backup_dir/secrets/acme.json"
    ok "  acme.json"
  fi

  # ── User data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Backing up user data..."
  mkdir -p "$backup_dir/data/users"
  if [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users" ]; then
    if is_btrfs "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users"; then
      local snap_users="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/.backup-snap-users-$$"
      btrfs_snapshot_r "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users" "$snap_users"
      rsync -a --exclude='*/home/.docker' "$snap_users/" "$backup_dir/data/users/"
      btrfs_delete "$snap_users"
    else
      rsync -a --exclude='*/home/.docker' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users/" "$backup_dir/data/users/"
    fi
    ok "  users/"
  fi

  # ── Sandbox volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  if [ "$skip_sandbox_volumes" = false ]; then
    info "Backing up sandbox volumes..."
    mkdir -p "$backup_dir/data/sandboxes"
    if [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes" ]; then
      if is_btrfs "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes"; then
        local snap_sandboxes="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/.backup-snap-sandboxes-$$"
        btrfs_snapshot_r "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes" "$snap_sandboxes"
        rsync -a "$snap_sandboxes/" "$backup_dir/data/sandboxes/"
        btrfs_delete "$snap_sandboxes"
      else
        rsync -a "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes/" "$backup_dir/data/sandboxes/"
      fi
      ok "  sandboxes/"
    fi
  else
    info "Skipping sandbox volumes (--no-sandbox-volumes)"
  fi

  # ── Snapshot dirs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  if [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/snapshots" ]; then
    info "Backing up snapshot directories..."
    mkdir -p "$backup_dir/data/snapshots"
    rsync -a "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/snapshots/" "$backup_dir/data/snapshots/"
    ok "  snapshots/"
  fi

  # ── Snapshot Docker images ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  if [ "$skip_snapshot_images" = false ]; then
    local snap_images
    snap_images=$($DOCKER images --format '{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}' 2>/dev/null \
      | grep '^sc-snap-' || true)
    if [ -n "$snap_images" ]; then
      info "Saving snapshot Docker images..."
      mkdir -p "$backup_dir/images"
      local img fname
      while IFS= read -r img; do
        fname=$(echo "$img" | tr '/:' '-').tar
        info "  docker save $img..."
        $DOCKER save "$img" > "$backup_dir/images/$fname"
        ok "  $fname"
      done <<< "$snap_images"
    fi
  else
    info "Skipping snapshot images (--no-snapshot-images)"
  fi

  # ── Manifest ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  local user_count sandbox_count snapshot_count
  user_count=$(find "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo 0)
  sandbox_count=$(find "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo 0)
  snapshot_count=$(find "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/snapshots" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo 0)

  local includes='"db","secrets","user_data"'
  [ "$skip_sandbox_volumes" = false ] && includes="$includes,\"sandbox_volumes\""
  [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/snapshots" ] && includes="$includes,\"snapshot_dirs\""
  [ "$skip_snapshot_images" = false ] && includes="$includes,\"snapshot_images\""

content.content:
cat > "$backup_dir/manifest.json" <<MANIFEST
{
  "version": "${version}",
  "schema_version": 1,
  "created_at": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "hostname": "$(hostname)",
  "includes": [${includes}],
  "user_count": ${user_count},
  "sandbox_count": ${sandbox_count},
  "snapshot_count": ${snapshot_count}
}
MANIFEST

  # ── Create archive ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Creating archive..."
  tar --use-compress-program=zstd -cf "$output" -C "$work_dir" sandcastle-backup

  local size
  size=$(du -sh "$output" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1 || echo "?")

  echo ""
  ok "Backup complete: $output (${size})"
  echo ""
}

# ═══ cmd_restore ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cmd_restore() {
  local backup_file=""
  local skip_db=false
  local skip_data=false
  local skip_images=false
  local yes=false

  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --skip-db)     skip_db=true; shift ;;
      --skip-data)   skip_data=true; shift ;;
      --skip-images) skip_images=true; shift ;;
      --yes|-y)      yes=true; shift ;;
      -*)            die "Unknown option: $1 (run 'sandcastle-admin help' for usage)" ;;
      *)             backup_file="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
  done

  [ -n "$backup_file" ] || die "Usage: sandcastle-admin restore <backup-file.tar.zst> [options]"
  [ -f "$backup_file" ] || die "Backup file not found: $backup_file"

  command -v zstd >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "zstd not found — install: apt-get install zstd"

  # ── Read and validate manifest ────────────────────────────────────────────

  local work_dir
  work_dir=$(mktemp -d)
  trap "rm -rf '$work_dir'" EXIT

  info "Reading backup manifest..."
  tar --use-compress-program=zstd -xf "$backup_file" -C "$work_dir" \
    sandcastle-backup/manifest.json 2>/dev/null \
    || die "Cannot read manifest — is this a valid Sandcastle backup file?"

  local manifest="$work_dir/sandcastle-backup/manifest.json"

  # Parse with python3 (always available on Ubuntu)
  local bk_version bk_created bk_hostname bk_schema bk_users bk_sandboxes bk_snapshots
  bk_version=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('version','?'))")
  bk_created=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('created_at','?'))")
  bk_hostname=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('hostname','?'))")
  bk_schema=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('schema_version',1))")
  bk_users=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('user_count',0))")
  bk_sandboxes=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('sandbox_count',0))")
  bk_snapshots=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$manifest')); print(d.get('snapshot_count',0))")

  if [ "${bk_schema}" -gt 1 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    warn "Backup schema_version=${bk_schema} is newer than supported (1) — proceeding anyway"
  fi

  echo ""
  echo -e "${BLUE}═══ Sandcastle Restore ═══${NC}"
  echo ""
  echo -e "  Backup version:  ${YELLOW}${bk_version}${NC}"
  echo -e "  Created:         ${bk_created}"
  echo -e "  Source host:     ${bk_hostname}"
  echo -e "  Users:           ${bk_users}"
  echo -e "  Sandboxes:       ${bk_sandboxes}"
  echo -e "  Snapshots:       ${bk_snapshots}"
  echo ""
  echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: This will overwrite all current Sandcastle data!${NC}"
  echo ""

  if [ "$yes" != true ]; then
    read -rp "Type 'yes' to confirm: " CONFIRM
    [ "$CONFIRM" = "yes" ] || die "Aborted"
  fi

  # ── Step 1: Stop Sandcastle ───────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Step 1/7: Stopping Sandcastle..."
  cd "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"
  $DOCKER compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" down 2>/dev/null || true
  ok "Services stopped"

  # ── Extract backup archive ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Extracting backup archive..."
  tar --use-compress-program=zstd -xf "$backup_file" -C "$work_dir"
  local bk_dir="$work_dir/sandcastle-backup"

  # ── Step 2: Restore secrets ───────────────────────────────────────────────

  info "Step 2/7: Restoring secrets..."

  local main_env="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"

  if [ -f "$bk_dir/secrets/rails.secrets" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails"
    cp "$bk_dir/secrets/rails.secrets" "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
    chmod 600 "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
    # Inject AR keys into .env
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$bk_dir/secrets/rails.secrets"
    set +a
    local key val
    for key in AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT; do
      val="${!key:-}"
      if [ -n "$val" ]; then
        if grep -q "^${key}=" "$main_env" 2>/dev/null; then
          sed -i "s|^${key}=.*|${key}=${val}|" "$main_env"
        else
          echo "${key}=${val}" >> "$main_env"
        fi
      fi
    done
    ok "  rails.secrets"
  fi

  if [ -f "$bk_dir/secrets/postgres.secrets" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres"
    cp "$bk_dir/secrets/postgres.secrets" "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres/.secrets"
    chmod 600 "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres/.secrets"
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$bk_dir/secrets/postgres.secrets"
    set +a
    if [ -n "${DB_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
      if grep -q "^DB_PASSWORD=" "$main_env" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "s|^DB_PASSWORD=.*|DB_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD}|" "$main_env"
      else

     9→    # snapshot_path: destination (e.g. /data/snapshots/alice/mysnap/home)
    10→    def snapshot_subvolume(source_path, snapshot_path)

    17→      output, status = run_sudo_command("/usr/bin/btrfs subvolume snapshot -r #{source_path} #{snapshot_path}")

    22→      Rails.logger.info("Created BTRFS snapshot: #{source_path} → #{snapshot_path}")

    24→    rescue Error
    25→      raise
    26→    rescue StandardError => e
    27→      raise Error, "Snapshot failed: #{e.message}"
    28→    end
    29→
    30→    # Delete a snapshot subvolume
    31→    def delete_snapshot(snapshot_path)
    32→      return false unless Dir.exist?(snapshot_path)
    33→
    34→      output, status = run_sudo_command("/usr/bin/btrfs subvolume delete #{snapshot_path}")
    35→      unless status&.success?
    36→        raise Error, "Failed to delete BTRFS snapshot #{snapshot_path}: #{output}"
    37→      end
    38→
    39→      Rails.logger.info("Deleted BTRFS snapshot: #{snapshot_path}")

    41→    rescue Error
    42→      raise
    43→    rescue StandardError => e
    44→      raise Error, "Snapshot deletion failed: #{e.message}"
    45→    end
    46→
    47→    # Get the size in bytes of a subvolume (approximate, from exclusive bytes used)
    48→    def subvolume_size(path)
    49→      return 0 unless Dir.exist?(path)
    50→
    51→      output, status = run_sudo_command("/usr/bin/btrfs subvolume show #{path}")
    52→      return 0 unless status&.success?
    53→
    54→      # Try to parse "Exclusive referenced:" from output
    55→      if (match = output.match(/Exclusive referenced:\s+([\d.]+)\s*(\w+)/i))
    56→        value = match[1].to_f
    57→        unit  = match[2].downcase
    58→        case unit
    59→        when "kib" then (value * 1024).to_i
    60→        when "mib" then (value * 1024 * 1024).to_i
    61→        when "gib" then (value * 1024 * 1024 * 1024).to_i
    62→        when "tib" then (value * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024).to_i
    63→        else value.to_i
    64→        end
    65→      else
    66→        0
    67→      end
    68→    rescue StandardError

    70→    end
    71→
    72→    # Restore: create a writable subvolume from a read-only snapshot
    73→    # snapshot_path: read-only snapshot source
    74→    # target_path: where to create the writable copy (must not exist)
    75→    def restore_subvolume(snapshot_path, target_path)
    76→      return false unless btrfs?
    77→      return false unless Dir.exist?(snapshot_path)
    78→
    79→      # Remove target if it exists so we can restore cleanly
    80→      if Dir.exist?(target_path)

    83→        else
    84→          FileUtils.rm_rf(target_path)
    85→        end
    86→      end
    87→
    88→      parent = File.dirname(target_path)
    89→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(parent) unless Dir.exist?(parent)
    90→
    91→      output, status = run_sudo_command("/usr/bin/btrfs subvolume snapshot #{snapshot_path} #{target_path}")
    92→      unless status&.success?
    93→        raise Error, "Failed to restore BTRFS snapshot to #{target_path}: #{output}"
    94→      end
    95→
    96→      ensure_owned(target_path)
    97→      Rails.logger.info("Restored BTRFS snapshot: #{snapshot_path} → #{target_path}")

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary: Backup, Export, and Snapshot Functionality in Sandcastle

Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here's what I found regarding backup, export, snapshot, and data persistence:

### 1. **Snapshot Functionality** (Container + Filesystem)
The most advanced feature for preserving sandbox state:

**Location:** 
- CLI: `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/snapshot.go`
- Rails Service: `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/sandbox_manager.rb` (lines 349-432)
- Model: `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/snapshot.rb`
- API: `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/snapshots_controller.rb`
- Jobs: `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/jobs/snapshot_create_job.rb`, `snapshot_destroy_job.rb`

**What it captures:**

| File | Purpose |

| `/app/models/snapshot.rb` | Snapshot DB model (docker_image, home_snapshot, data_snapshot, sizes) |

| `/installer.sh` | System backup/restore via `sandcastle-admin` |

| `/app/services/btrfs_helper.rb` | BTRFS snapshot/restore operations |
| `/db/migrate/20260302000*.rb` | Archive retention schema |
| `/config/recurring.yml` | Daily 3am archive purge schedule |

There are three levels of backup in Sandcastle:

### 1. Sandbox Snapshots (individual sandbox)
```bash
sandcastle snapshot create <sandbox> <name>
sandcastle snapshot list
sandcastle snapshot restore <sandbox> <snapshot>
```
Captures the container layer (Docker commit) plus BTRFS snapshots of home and data volumes. Near-instant if on BTRFS.

### 2. Archive & Restore (soft-delete)
When you destroy a sandbox, it's archived by default — volume data is preserved for the retention period (default 30 days).
```bash
sandcastle list --archived
sandcastle unarchive <id>
```

### 3. Full Instance Backup (entire Sandcastle installation)
```bash
sandcastle-admin backup                    # creates .tar.zst
sandcastle-admin backup --output /path/to  # custom output path
sandcastle-admin restore backup-file.tar.zst
```
Includes all 4 Postgres databases, secrets, user home/data directories, snapshots, snapshot Docker images, and TLS certs. Can also be used during reinstall:
```bash
sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
```

For a typical "I want to protect my work" use case, **snapshots** are the way to go. For disaster recovery of the whole server, use `sandcastle-admin backup`.
